---
name: aidlc-ux-design-skill
description: |
  The skill of designing user interfaces — screen layouts, navigation flows, information architecture, and interaction patterns. Translates requirements and stories into visual structure that developers can implement.
---

# UX Design

## Purpose

Design the UI surface of a system — what screens exist, how they connect, what data they show, and how users interact with them.

## Principles

- Start from user journeys, not features — map the flow a user takes to accomplish a goal
- Every screen must have a clear purpose — if you can't state it in one sentence, split it
- Navigation should be obvious — users should never be lost or unable to go back
- Data density matches the user's task — dashboards are dense, forms are focused
- Consistency across screens — same patterns for same interactions
- Responsive by default — consider how layouts adapt across screen sizes
- Accessibility is not optional — labels, contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
- Error states and empty states are real screens — design them, don't leave them to developers

## Application

When producing wireframe artifacts:
- Read requirements and stories to understand what the system does
- Identify all screens needed (one per user goal or major action)
- Define the navigation map (how screens connect)
- For each screen: what data is displayed, what data is submitted, what actions are available
- Document layout guidance — element placement, sizing, responsive behaviour
- Reference source stories for traceability
